name: Red Pepper Pickle
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the red pepper in half and remove the seeds and white membrane.
2. Slice the pepper as thinly as possible, then cut the longer slices into halves.
3. Place the pepper slices in a non-metal bowl to prevent any metallic taste from lemon juice reacting with metal surfaces.
4. Shave very thin slices off the onion top end using a sharp knife; aim for wafer-thin slices and separate the rings.
5. Mix the onion slices with the pepper slices, adjusting the quantity of onion per pepper as desired (e.g., 1/8 small onion to a medium red bell pepper).
6. Add the lemon juice, freshly ground black pepper to taste, and cumin seeds.
7. Cover the bowl and let it sit for at least one hour, stirring occasionally; this can be done at room temperature or even in direct sunlight.
This recipe serves small quantities (about 2 tablespoons per person) as a crisp complement to tender vegetables and creamy dishes.
